name: Wouter
description: Minimalist ~2KB hook-based router for small SPAs

name.comment: Official name as used in the repository and npm package.

description.value: A minimalist-friendly ~2.2KB routing for React and Preact that relies on Hooks

website.comment: Wouter has no dedicated docs site; the README serves as the documentation.

category.comment: Drop-in hook-based router you add to an existing React/Preact app; not a framework.

active-maintenance.comment: Last push 2026-07-10 and v3.9.0 released 2025-12-18 — well within the last ~6 months.

initial-release.comment: Repository created 2019-04-09; first public releases followed in 2019.

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: npm downloads for the week of 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-13.

routing-paradigm.comment: Routes declared with <Route>/<Switch> components; navigation and matching driven by hooks (useLocation, useRoute, useParams).

nested-layout-routes.comment: Supports nested routing via the <Router> base path and the <Route nest> prop for shared parent layouts.

dynamic-params.comment: Named path params like /users/:id, read via useParams.

optional-catch-all-params.comment: Optional segments (/:locale?/home) and wildcard/catch-all patterns (/app/*) are supported.

search-params-api.comment: First-class useSearch and useSearchParams hooks for reading/writing query-string state.

programmatic-navigation.comment: useLocation returns a setter for imperative navigation; also useBrowserLocation.

route-guards-middleware.comment: No built-in per-route guards, middleware, or beforeLoad-style hooks; guarding is done manually in components.

written-in-typescript.comment: Library source is authored in JavaScript; it ships hand-maintained TypeScript declaration (.d.ts) files rather than being written in TS.

type-safe-paths-links.comment: <Link>/navigate targets are plain strings and are not checked against a known set of routes.

type-safe-params.comment: useParams accepts an explicit generic but does not infer param names/types from the route path pattern; params default to string.

type-safe-search-params.comment: Search params are read as raw strings; no typed/validated search-param schema.

codegen-required.comment: No route-type generation or build step; typing is inference/declaration based.

type-safety-rating.comment: TS types are shipped but paths/params/search are not checked against routes — matches guideline rating 2.

route-data-loaders.comment: No loader / useLoaderData-style route-level data loading; fetching is done inside components.

actions-mutations.comment: No built-in action/mutation or form-handling primitive tied to routes.

prefetching.comment: No built-in route code/data prefetching on hover/intent.

pending-transition-ui.comment: No built-in pending/transition UI. v3.9 adds View Transitions API integration, but that is a browser transition effect, not a React pending-state API.

scroll-restoration.comment: No built-in scroll restoration across navigations.

per-route-code-splitting.comment: No dedicated per-route lazy API; a route can render a React.lazy component manually, but the router provides no code-splitting primitive.

per-route-error-handling.comment: No route-scoped error element/boundary; error handling relies on standard React error boundaries.

ssr-support.comment: Supported but DIY: <Router ssrPath> / ssrSearch let you render on the server, but you must wire up your own server and hydration.

streaming-ssr.comment: No first-class streaming/suspenseful SSR feature; the router is renderable but streaming is the surrounding stack's concern.

react-server-components.comment: Hook/context based client router; no first-class RSC support.

static-site-generation.comment: No built-in static pre-rendering; SSG would depend entirely on an external framework/toolchain.

client-only-usable.comment: Primary use case is a pure client-side SPA router with no server required.

framework-bound.comment: Drop-in library that works in any React/Preact app; no bundled build/framework toolchain.

bundle-size.comment: Minified + gzipped size of the wouter package per Bundlephobia (~2.6 KB); README markets ~2.1-2.2 KB.

zero-runtime-dependencies.comment: README markets 'zero dependencies', but the package pulls in two small runtime deps: regexparam and use-sync-external-store.

tree-shakeable.comment: Ships ES modules and exposes granular hooks (e.g. useBrowserLocation ~650B), supporting dead-code elimination.

documentation-quality.comment: Thorough single-page README with many runnable examples and an FAQ, but no dedicated docs site, guides, or migration section — adequate docs with some gaps (rating 3).

official-devtools.comment: No dedicated route/navigation devtools or inspector.

data-fetching-integration.comment: No first-class data-fetching integration; data fetching is left entirely to the app.

predecessor-lineage.comment: No predecessor project; wouter is an original minimalist router by Alexey Taktarov (molefrog).
